questions.question: An object has a velocity vector $\mathbf{v} = \begin{pmatrix} 3 \ -4 \end{pmatrix}$ m/s. Calculate its speed.
questions.explanation:
Speed is the magnitude of the velocity vector: $\sqrt{3^2 + (-4)^2} = \sqrt{25} = 5$.

**Common mistake:** Adding the components directly (getting 7) or ignoring the negative sign.

questions.question: A particle starts at position $\mathbf{r}_0 = \begin{pmatrix} 2 \ 5 \end{pmatrix}$ and moves with velocity $\mathbf{v} = \begin{pmatrix} 1 \ 3 \end{pmatrix}$ for 2 seconds. Find its new position.
questions.explanation:
$\mathbf{r} = \mathbf{r}_0 + \mathbf{v}t = \begin{pmatrix} 2 \ 5 \end{pmatrix} + 2\begin{pmatrix} 1 \ 3 \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} 2+2 \ 5+6 \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} 4 \ 11 \end{pmatrix}$.

**Common mistake:** Only calculating the displacement $\mathbf{v}t$ (2i + 6j) and forgetting to add the initial position.

questions.question: If an object moves from $\begin{pmatrix} 0 \ 0 \end{pmatrix}$ to $\begin{pmatrix} 12 \ 16 \end{pmatrix}$ in 4 seconds at constant velocity, find its velocity vector.
questions.explanation:
Velocity $\mathbf{v} = \text{Displacement} / \text{time} = \begin{pmatrix} 12/4 \ 16/4 \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} 3 \ 4 \end{pmatrix}$.

**Common mistake:** Multiplying the displacement by time instead of dividing.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the direction of travel for an object with velocity $\mathbf{v} = 2\mathbf{i} + 2\mathbf{j}$?
questions.explanation:
Since the i and j components are equal and positive, the vector points at 45° to the positive x-axis.

**Common mistake:** Confusing the axis orientations or failing to recognize the 1:1 ratio.

questions.question: Find the displacement of a ship moving with velocity $\begin{pmatrix} 10 \ -5 \end{pmatrix}$ km/h over a period of 3 hours.
questions.explanation:
Displacement $\mathbf{s} = \mathbf{v}t = 3 \times \begin{pmatrix} 10 \ -5 \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} 30 \ -15 \end{pmatrix}$.

**Common mistake:** Dividing the velocity by time.
